content: "followed by spaceS" implies that there are cases with several white spaces. That is currently not the case, also, maybe the "thing" could be "my" too

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Elle respecte toutes les règles, j'ai fait cette solution :

sentence = input()

s = sentence.split()

res = ""

for elt in s:
    if elt in ["my", "MY", "My", "mY"]:
        res = "Our " + s[s.index(elt)+1]
        break

while res[-1] in [".", "!", "?", ","]:
    res = res[:-1]

res += "."

print(res)
I wonder if the minimum sentence length should not rather be greater than or equal to 4, since it contains at least “My x”?

Constraints & Stub generator
* For the shortest mode, it may be preferable to use 98 / 99 instead of 255 (for programming languages that require strings to have a fixed length).

Cases
* Similar to your other contribution, please include words that start with "my" and words that end with "my" in some of the cases.

Thanks.
Sorry for the quick succession in puzzles, had the idea for this one while writing the tests and validators for Megapuzzle and couldn't wait to suggest it. :')
Done! Added three new test/validator pairs; must be said, making sentences with the words I found containing "my" is a bit of a challenge :')
Haha, a bit pedantic here but fine, changed from "0 <" to "4 ≤" ;-)

I bet it is! However, I can see that you've tried very well, or even a bit too well because there are two "my"s in Mynd games (Validator 7). Please fix that 😉

Also: well done! you changed "€" to "euros"! It's definitely the right thing to do; I saw it yesterday but I forgot to point it out 😆
Thanks, updated! And yeah, I added the "[[sentence]] is ASCII-encoded" then when checking, I saw it and remembered it was UTF-8 / ISO-8859-15 xD
